title: Bowen's disease - NHS
text:
hes removed at your GP surgery 5 to 14 days later or once your wound has healed. Sometimes dissolvable stitches are used that do not need to be removed.
If you had a dressing on your wound, you may need to go to your GP or the hospital to have it changed.
After treatment:
see a GP if an existing patch starts to bleed, change in appearance or develops a lump – do not wait for your follow-up appointment see a GP if you notice any new patches on your skin make sure you protect your skin from the sun – wear protective clothing and use a sunscreen with a high sun protection factor (SPF) of at least 30 Get more advice on sunscreen and sun safety

Next review due: 06 October 2025
